text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build and running tests. This process, managed through a CI pipeline, helps catch issues early before they become bigger problems. For example, every time a developer submits new code, the system automatically compiles it and runs a suite of tests. If something breaks, the team is notified immediately. This saves time and reduces bugs, making collaboration smoother and the final product more reliable.
